Perplexity AI for Government

AI-Powered Research for California State Employees

Core Thesis: Perplexity gives you research-grade answers with cited sources in seconds — and your .gov email gets you Pro access for free.

February 2026

Why Perplexity?

Perplexity is an AI-powered research engine that searches the internet and delivers answers with cited sources — so you can verify every claim.

Traditional Search (Google)

  • Returns a list of links
  • You read each page yourself
  • You synthesize the answer
  • Time: 10-30 minutes per question

Perplexity

  • Returns a direct answer
  • Reads and synthesizes pages for you
  • Every claim has an inline citation
  • Time: 10-30 seconds per question

Tip: Think of Perplexity as a research assistant that reads the internet for you and shows its work.

How is this different from ChatGPT?

ChatGPT generates answers from its training data — it can be creative but may "hallucinate" facts. Perplexity searches the live web for every query and cites real sources. You can click any citation to verify the original page.

Feature ChatGPT Perplexity
Live web search Optional (plugin) Every query
Source citations Rare Every claim
Real-time data Training cutoff Always current
Best for Creative writing, coding Research, fact-finding

Getting Your Free Account

As a California state employee, your .gov email unlocks Perplexity Pro for free — the same tier that costs $20/month for everyone else.

Step 1

Go to Perplexity for Government and click Sign Up

Step 2

Create an account using your .ca.gov email address

Step 3

A banner will appear on your homepage — click it to redeem your free Pro year

Done

You now have Pro features: advanced AI models, 300+ searches/day, file analysis, and Deep Research

Security note: All .gov accounts get automatic zero data usage protection — your queries and uploads are never used to train AI models. No settings to change; it's on by default.
What if I don't see the banner?
  • Make sure you signed up with your .ca.gov email (not personal email)
  • Try logging out and back in
  • Check that your email is verified
  • If the banner still doesn't appear, your agency may need to enroll through the GSA Enterprise agreement — contact your IT department

The Interface

1 2 3 4 5 6

1New Thread — start a fresh conversation
2Spaces — organized project hubs (more on this later)
3Search Bar — type your question here in plain English
4+ Button — upload files, access Deep Research
5Model Selector — choose which AI answers (default is fine)
6Voice Input — microphone for speaking your question

Writing Better Prompts

The quality of your answer depends on the quality of your question. A little effort upfront saves significant time.

Good prompts are specific, provide context, and state what you need:

  • "What are the current CalHR telework policies for state employees, and how have they changed since 2024?"
  • "Compare California's water recycling regulations with Arizona's. Focus on agricultural reuse."
  • "Summarize the key provisions of Executive Order N-30-25 regarding state government innovation."

Pattern: [What you want] + [specific topic] + [constraints or focus area]

Vague prompts get vague answers:

  • "Tell me about telework" → Too broad. Which state? Which policies?
  • "Water regulations" → A book could be written. What aspect? Which jurisdiction?
  • "Innovation fellowship" → Which one? What about it?

Why they fail: The AI has to guess what you care about, and it usually guesses wrong.

Click the microphone icon in the search bar to speak your question. Voice works well for:

  • Quick questions while multitasking
  • Complex questions that are easier to say than type
  • Accessibility needs

Tip: Speak naturally — you don't need special syntax. "What are the latest CalPERS retirement benefit changes?" works perfectly.

Example Prompts for Government Work

Policy Research

  • "What are California's current requirements for state agency AI use under the Governor's executive orders?"
  • "Summarize SB 1047 and its implications for state technology procurement."

Data Lookup

  • "What is California's current unemployment rate compared to the national average?"
  • "How much did California spend on Medi-Cal in fiscal year 2024-25?"

Comparative Analysis

  • "Compare California's approach to homelessness funding with New York's. Include dollar amounts."
  • "What are the pros and cons of centralized vs. decentralized IT governance in state government?"

Document Analysis

  • Upload a PDF and ask: "Summarize the key action items from this meeting agenda."
  • Upload a report and ask: "What are the 3 most significant findings in this audit?"

Your First Search

Here's what a search result looks like. Perplexity searched 15 sources and synthesized a direct answer with inline citations.

1 2 3 4 5

1View Tabs — switch between the AI answer, source links, and images
2Share — share this thread with colleagues
3Source Count — how many pages Perplexity reviewed
4Inline Citation — click any citation to see the source and excerpt
5Follow-Up Bar — ask a follow-up question to dig deeper

Tip: Always click the citation numbers to verify sources, especially for facts you plan to include in official documents. Trust but verify.

Deep Research

Deep Research is Perplexity's most powerful feature. It spends up to 3 minutes doing multi-step research — reading dozens of sources, cross-referencing facts, and building a comprehensive report.

Regular Search Deep Research
Speed 10-30 seconds Up to 3 minutes
Daily Quota 300+ searches Limited (resets midnight)
Best For Quick lookups Policy analysis, reports
Sources Read ~5-10 pages 20-50+ pages

How to access: Click the + button in the search bar, then select Deep Research.

Tip: Use Deep Research when you'd normally spend 1-2 hours reading multiple sources. Use regular search for quick questions.

Choosing a Model

Perplexity lets you choose which AI model answers your question via the Model dropdown in the search bar.

Recommendation: Leave it on "Best" — Perplexity automatically picks the right model for each query. Only change it if you have a specific reason.

Models marked Max require the paid Max tier ($200/mo) and aren't included in the free government Pro plan.

When You Might Change Models
Model Best For
Best (default) Everyday use — auto-selects the optimal model
Sonar Fast answers from Perplexity's own model
GPT-5.2 Strong reasoning and analysis
Claude Sonnet 4.5 Nuanced writing and detailed explanations
Gemini 3 Pro Google's model — good with data and facts
Grok 4.1 Creative approaches and brainstorming

Spaces & Collaboration

Spaces are project hubs where you can organize research by topic, add reference files, set custom instructions, and collaborate with colleagues.

1 2 3 4 5 6

1Space Title — name your project or topic
2Description — what this Space is for
3Search Bar — searches within this Space's context
4Files — upload PDFs, docs, or images as reference material
5Instructions — custom AI behavior for this Space
6Share — invite colleagues as viewers or contributors
Ideas for Government Spaces
  • Legislative Tracking — Upload bill texts, set instructions like "Always include the bill number and current status"
  • Budget Analysis — Add budget documents as context, ask questions across all uploaded files
  • Policy Research — Create a Space per policy area with relevant reports and data
  • Team Projects — Share with your team so everyone's research builds on the same foundation
  • Meeting Prep — Upload agendas and background materials before a meeting

Privacy & Security

This is the most important slide for government employees. Perplexity handles your data carefully — but you should know where the controls are.

Finding Your Settings

Click your profile icon (bottom-left corner), then select Preferences.

The Key Setting: AI Data Retention

In Preferences, scroll to Artificial Intelligence and find the AI data retention toggle.

For .gov accounts: Your data is already protected automatically. The AI data retention toggle should be OFF — meaning your searches are not used to train Perplexity's models. Verify this is off the first time you log in.

Warning: Do not upload documents containing PII (personally identifiable information), PHI (protected health information), classified material, or credentials. While Perplexity doesn't train on .gov data, uploaded files are processed on Perplexity's servers. When in doubt, ask your information security officer.

File Upload Rules
  • Uploaded files are auto-deleted after 7 days
  • Your conversation threads are not deleted (only the files)
  • Don't upload: SSNs, medical records, passwords, API keys, internal-only documents marked confidential
  • Safe to upload: Published reports, public data, meeting agendas, legislation text, your own draft documents for review

Voice & Mobile

Perplexity works on your phone too — and voice input makes research hands-free.

Voice Input (Desktop): Click the microphone icon in the search bar and speak your question naturally. Works in Chrome, Edge, and Safari.

Mobile Apps: Available for iOS and Android. Same Pro features as desktop — search, Deep Research, Spaces, and file uploads all work on mobile.

When voice is useful:

  • Dictating complex questions while reviewing documents
  • Quick lookups during meetings (with earbuds)
  • Accessibility — if typing is difficult, voice is a full-featured alternative

Tip: The mobile app supports voice input too, making it great for research on the go between meetings.

Tips for Government Work

Start every session with a clear question. The more specific your prompt, the better the answer. Include agency names, policy numbers, date ranges, and what format you want the answer in.

Always verify citations. Perplexity cites its sources — click them. For anything going into official documents, verify the original source says what Perplexity claims it says.

Use Deep Research for complex analysis. If you'd normally spend an hour reading multiple sources, Deep Research can do it in 3 minutes. Perfect for legislative analysis, policy comparisons, and background research.

Create Spaces for ongoing projects. Don't start from scratch every session. A Space remembers your uploaded files and custom instructions across conversations.

Know the limitations:

  • Perplexity cannot access your agency's intranet or internal systems
  • It may not have access to paywalled academic journals or proprietary databases
  • Answers should complement, not replace, official research processes
  • AI can make mistakes — always apply professional judgment
Common Pitfalls to Avoid
  • Trusting without verifying — Citations exist for a reason. Click them.
  • Uploading sensitive data — Keep PII, PHI, and confidential documents off cloud AI tools.
  • Using AI output verbatim — Rephrase and verify before including in official communications.
  • Forgetting context — Perplexity doesn't know your agency's internal policies unless you tell it.
  • Ignoring Deep Research — Many Fellows only use basic search and miss the most powerful feature.
Sample Prompts by Role

Policy Analyst

  • "Compare the enforcement mechanisms in SB 1047 vs AB 2013 for AI regulation in California."
  • "What federal funding streams are available for state broadband infrastructure in 2026?"

Program Manager

  • "What are best practices for measuring government program outcomes? Include examples from other states."
  • "Summarize the latest OMB guidance on evidence-based policymaking."

IT / Technology Staff

  • "What are the FedRAMP-authorized alternatives to [specific tool] for state government use?"
  • "Compare cloud migration strategies for legacy state government systems. Include cost estimates."

Communications / Public Affairs

  • "What are the top 3 issues California residents are concerned about regarding [topic], based on recent polling?"
  • "Draft talking points about [initiative] — focus on public benefit and taxpayer value."
